habitat: High altitude rocky areas; cliffs; river valleys; and rocky slopes from 2000–5200 m
story: An icon of the skies, the Snow Pigeon (Columba leuconota) is a species from the Columbidae family. It thrives in High altitude rocky areas; cliffs; river valleys; and rocky slopes from 2000–5200 m, where it interacts with its environment in unique ways. With a conservation status of Least Concern (LC), the Snow Pigeon is a focus for conservationists worldwide. Understanding the needs of the Snow Pigeon helps us better comprehend the challenges facing our planet's wildlife. This poster is a celebration of the Snow Pigeon, a bird that inspires wonder and highlights the critical importance of conservation efforts.
Every poster is crafted to inspire curiosity about our planet's incredible biodiversity.
